title: Preparation and Handling of Anaerobic Bacteria for Mouse Oral Gavage
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ba2k9","text":"This protocol outlines the set up and transfer of anaerobic bacterial culture from an anaerobic chamber for the oral administration of bacteria to animals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bb9fi","text":"anaerobic chamber","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4pg86","text":"cryotubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6a1bk","text":"parafilm","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1dnbl","text":"grow flasks","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"82c2o","text":"1ml slip-tip syringes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33puf","text":"plastic gavage tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"52obi","text":"GasPack box (Anaerobe systems)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"17d5m","text":"AnaeroPack - Anaero sachets (MCG)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8j2q","text":"pre-reduced 50% glycerol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bj4rf","text":"pre-reduced PBS with 1.5% NaHCO3","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grow anaerobic bacteria inside an anaerobic chamber (80% nitrogen, 10% carbon dioxide, and 10% hydrogen) at 37℃ to an approximate optical density (600nm) of 1
Centrifuge culture and resuspend in sterile pre-reduced 50% glycerol to an approximate Colony-Forming Unit (CFU) density of 10 8 /mL inside the chamber
For extremely oxygen sensitive bacteria, wrap cryotubes with parafilm prior to removing from anaerobic chamber
For extremely oxygen sensitive bacteria, wrap growth flasks with parafilm prior to removing from anaerobic chamber
Store aliquots at -20℃ for short-term storage, -80℃ for long-term storage
Thaw desired aliquots while preparing gavage supplies
Attach gavage needles to syringes (inside a hood if sterility is of concern) and place inside GasPack box
Place an AnaeroPack - Anaero sachet inside GasPack box, DO NOT OPEN the sachet until inside the anaerobic chamber
Transfer supplies and aliquots into anaerobic chamber
Transfer aliquots to conical tubes and centrifuge to remove glycerol
Resuspend culture in pre-reduced PBS with 1.5% NaHCO3
Draw up culture into gavage syringes
Place ready gavage syringes and opened AnaeroPack-Anaero sachet in GasPack box, seal tightly
Remove from chamber and transport to animal facility

For extremely oxygen sensitive bacteria, open the GasPack box as minimally as possible during gavage process and administer gavage as quickly as safely possible to animals to reduce oxygen exposure times
creator: Anastasiya Moiseyenko
